title: Scalability: Add MaxInputFindings limit for theme extraction
body:
## Summary

The theme extraction prompt includes all prior findings without a limit. For long-running projects with extensive review history, this could exceed LLM context limits or incur excessive token costs.

## Current State

`buildComprehensivePrompt()` iterates over all findings in the triage context:
```go
for i, f := range findings {
    // All findings included in prompt
}
```

## Proposed Solution

Add a `MaxInputFindings` config option (default: 50-100) that limits extraction to the most recent N findings. This bounds prompt size while still providing sufficient context for theme extraction.

```go
// In ThemeExtractionConfig
MaxInputFindings int `yaml:"maxInputFindings"` // Default: 100
```

## Current Mitigations

1. Only recent triaged findings are loaded (not entire history)
2. `MinFindingsForTheme` provides a lower bound
3. Extraction model (Haiku) has generous context

These make this a low-priority issue, but worth addressing for projects with high review volume.

## Origin

Identified during PR #243 code review (finding `d7c3f33608c2893d3f9c1a6cebf56f9c`).
